anti_claim: Concurrency mission control verifies exact copied non-secret macro builder and public bridge artifact bodies plus a public Work Ledger seed-speed topology fixture. It does not authorize private mission-control runtime export, live scheduling, provider dispatch, hosted orchestration, production concurrency safety, publication, release, private-root equivalence, source mutation, private session export, or whole-system correctness.

allowed_public_inputs: exact copied non-secret concurrency mission-control macro builder body
exact copied public provider-canary and Task Ledger bridge artifacts
public Work Ledger seed-speed topology counts and synthetic session/claim cards
source refs to existing Work Ledger body-floor import surfaces
source refs, digests, line counts, and required anchors
public synthetic lane and bridge fixtures
stable negative-case ids and validator verdicts

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A fixture board inflated into live scheduler authority, production concurrency-safety evidence, hosted orchestration proof, provider dispatch, private ledger export, release approval, or a copied body without digest equality and negative cases.
failure_modes: source module digest drifts from macro source without a refreshed exact copy and manifest
copied source modules lack required anchors for transaction evaluation, provider repair, Task Ledger residual replay, or authority counters
validator accepts a missing seed root or blocked provider bridge
validator accepts authority-collapse or private runtime overclaims
validator accepts unresolved Work Ledger claim collisions in the seed-speed topology fixture
receipts include copied body text, raw operator transcripts, private refs, provider payload bodies, credentials, private Task Ledger rows, private Work Ledger session bodies, or hosted runtime state
capsule is treated as a live scheduler, production safety proof, provider dispatch, publication, release, private-root equivalence, source mutation, or whole-system correctness authority
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe exact-copy concurrency mission-control capsule for one macro builder body plus six public bridge artifacts, exercised over synthetic transaction lanes, bridge receipts, and a Work Ledger seed-speed topology fixture while preserving claim ceilings.
standard_payload.teleology: Let a cold reader inspect and rerun real concurrency mission-control mechanics without granting live scheduler, provider, hosted orchestration, publication, release, source-mutation, private-ledger, or private-root authority.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

validation_rules: require seven copied macro source modules
verify source-to-target digest equality and required anchors for the copied builder and bridge artifacts
execute the public concurrency mission-control exercise without invoking live agents, providers, browser/HUD state, hosted orchestration, or source mutation

require the Work Ledger seed-speed topology fixture to expose heartbeat, seed-speed status, mutation-check command refs, multi-session and active-claim counts, explicit heartbeat participation, and zero selected-session claim collisions
require stable negative-case codes for missing seed roots, provider bridge blockage, authority-collapse claims, private runtime claims, and unresolved Work Ledger seed-speed claim collisions

keep live scheduler claims, production concurrency-safety claims, provider dispatch, model dispatch, publication, release, private-root equivalence, source mutation, and whole-system correctness authority false
keep copied source bodies under source_modules or seed_root only; receipts may expose refs, digests, anchors, counts, verdicts, and omission receipts but not body text

author.purpose: create a valid Concurrency mission control instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Concurrency mission control without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_concurrency_mission_control and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

anti_claims: Concurrency mission control verifies exact copied non-secret macro builder and public bridge artifact bodies plus a public Work Ledger seed-speed topology fixture. It does not authorize private mission-control runtime export, live scheduling, provider dispatch, hosted orchestration, production concurrency safety, publication, release, private-root equivalence, source mutation, private session export, or whole-system correctness.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

runtime_acceptance_refs.uplift_basis: registry accepted_public_runtime_standard plus standard-local validator_contract, receipt_contract, source_refs, authority_ceiling, public_private_boundary, and anti-claim floor.
